free_text: Interaction of 80-164 MeV protons with nickel isotopes
Off-line activation measurements
Isotopically enriched Ni-58,Ni-60,Ni-62,Ni-64 targets
(about 1 mg/cm**2 in thickness) were used.
Ge(Li) crystal which had an efficiency of 10%
compared to a 7.6 cm x 7.6 cm NaI detector for the
1332 keV line of a l OCO source placed at 25 cm. Most
measurements were made with the Ge(Li) at 90. degr.,
3 to 5 cm from the target.
The Cascade and Hybrid Model.
The in-beam gamma-rays.
Observed gamma-rays were assigned to a
particular final nucleus on the basis of  the  measured
energies and  the  consistency  of  relative intensity
patterns with those expected for gamma-ray transitions
belonging  to  one  or  more cascades for that nucleus.
For this purpose a list of gamma rays assigned on the
basis  of in-beam measurements to nuclei with 44<=A<=65
and their relative location in their decay schemes  was
prepared from the recently published literature.
Needed correction for dead time of the electronics and
the data acquisition system has been included.
. Systematic uncertainties associated with the
absolute cross sections due to:
* charge integration,
* dead time correction,
* target thickness, and
* detection efficiency
are estimated to be about 15% but for the 80 MeV
measurements for Ni-58 and Ni-62 and 100 MeV
measurements with a Ni-64 target is estimated to be
30% because of a bias in charge integration due to an
alternating current pick up in the Faraday cup for the
first case and because of poor integration at low
currents (< 0.1 nA) employed in the second case.
*by Authors*
-Cross sections for the production of individual final
nuclei were obtained by summing observed cross sections
for gamma rays assigned to transition to the ground
state and to the known isomeric states of the
respective nucleus.
-It should be noted that the in-beam measurements
yield only a lower limit for the production cross-
sections for given nucleus since weak transitions to
and direct production of the ground state are not
observed.
Reaction lines are corrected.
Transformation to lower case. All '-G'
were deleted from reaction lines.
Last checking has been done.
SD: SF5=UND deleted from REACTION code in
several Subents, author's list update, 4-digits year
added in all relevant lines. ERR-ANALYS update (ERR-S
-> ERR-SYS, description was changed). En=140 -> 164 MeV
in several Subents.
SD: REL-REF updated.
VT+On. Revision in 031
No information about source of uncertainties
Compiler believes that they are relative uncertainties
described in the text: 'Relative uncertainties in the
cross sections vary widely and depend not only on the
statistics but often on the accuracy with which
overlapping peaks could be separated and, in a number
of cases, on the precision with which the delayed
components could be subtracted.

overlapping peaks could be separated and, in a number
of cases, on the precision with which the delayed
components could be subtracted.
